steps.title: Split the cube root
steps.content:
Use the product property of cube roots so each factor can be simplified separately.
$$\sqrt[3]{x^6\cdot y^3}=\sqrt[3]{x^6}\cdot\sqrt[3]{y^3}$$

steps.title: Simplify the powers
steps.content:
A cube root divides each exponent by $3$, so the powers reduce cleanly.
$$\sqrt[3]{x^6}=x^2$$
$$\sqrt[3]{y^3}=y$$
Multiplying the simplified factors gives $x^2y$, the same product shown in the choice. The answer is B.
